title: Giant Clipboard Wall Easel Chalkboard
summary: A wall-mounted chalkboard shaped and hinged like an oversized clipboard, with a working wood-dowel clip.
description:
The visual joke here — a chalkboard that's also a giant clipboard — depends on getting the clip mechanism right, and that's the one genuinely fiddly part of the build. The small arched dowel-holder pieces are too short to safely clamp and cut on their own, so the plan's trick is to cut a longer 16–20" strip first, shape the arch and drill the dowel hole on the overhanging end while the rest of the strip is still clamped down, and only then cut it to its final short length. Skipping that order and trying to clamp a 2"-long finished piece is how small parts go flying at the jigsaw.

Everything else is straightforward: two flat plywood plates, front and back, joined by the dowel-holder pieces at the top so a dowel rod can slide through and pin a stack of paper (or just decorate the piece) like a working clip. Round the back plate's corners if you want a softer look — purely cosmetic, no effect on function.

tools.note: Cuts the arched clip shapes and the plate outlines.
Matched to your dowel diameter for the clip hole.
For safely holding the small dowel-holder strip while you shape and drill its overhanging end.

materials.name: Plywood, particle board, or MDF, 1/2", 4x4 sheet
Dowel rod, 1/2"–3/4", 36 in
materials.note: Match the diameter to whatever drill or spade bit you have.

cutList.note: Shape and drill on an overhanging strip before cutting to this final length.

steps.title: Cut and paint the back plate
steps.body: Transfer the arch and center-hole layout onto the larger plywood piece — this becomes the back plate, and sets the overall clipboard silhouette. Cut it out carefully with a jigsaw and sand the edges smooth. Paint this face with chalkboard paint before moving on; it's much easier to get even coverage on a flat, unobstructed panel now than after the clip hardware is attached.
materials: Plywood, particle board, or MDF, 1/2", 4x4 sheet

steps.title: Shape the clip pieces safely
steps.body: Cut a strip about 2" wide and at least 16" long (20" is more comfortable to work with). Clamp one end to the table and shape the arch on the overhanging end with the jigsaw, then drill the dowel-hole center on that same overhanging section. Only after the arch is cut and the hole is drilled do you cut the piece down to its final short length — trying to clamp and shape an already-short piece is how small parts go flying. If you'd rather skip this step, 1x3 boards work as a simpler substitute for the clip pieces.
materials: Plywood, particle board, or MDF, 1/2", 4x4 sheet

steps.title: Attach the clip pieces to the back plate
steps.body: Attach the shaped dowel-holder pieces to the top of the back plate, using glue and nails or screws from the back, or 1/2" pocket holes with 1" pocket screws driven from the front.

steps.title: Repeat on the front plate
steps.body: Cut the front plate to its outline and repeat the same clip-piece attachment on it.
materials: Plywood, particle board, or MDF, 1/2", 4x4 sheet

steps.title: Assemble and mount
steps.body: Attach the completed front clip assembly to the back plate. Round the back plate's corners now if you'd like a softer look — purely cosmetic. Slide the dowel rod through the clip holes to complete the working clip, and mount the finished piece to the wall.

images.alt: Oversized wall-mounted chalkboard shaped like a clipboard with a working wood dowel clip
